intro: Quote Vault keeps one moderated, local quote library for Twitch, YouTube, Kick, and TikTok. Ordinary chat is never saved; only explicit quote submissions and bounded management history enter private add-on state.

sections.title: 1. Start here
sections.description: Choose where Quote Vault is available. It uses normalized chat and needs no separate platform triggers.
sections.notice: Recommended first test: keep community submissions enabled, submit one harmless quote, approve it as a moderator, then retrieve it from the same platform.

sections.title: 2. Viewer commands
sections.description: Public retrieval and moderated submission commands.

sections.title: 3. Moderator commands
sections.description: Trusted additions, review, editing, recoverable deletion, and statistics.

sections.title: 4. Safety and spam prevention
sections.description: Bots, exact ignores, links, sizes, queue capacity, and cooldowns.

sections.title: 5. Optional starter library
sections.description: Seed a new empty library without replacing existing quotes.
sections.notice: Use one line per item: Quoted Name | Quote text. This seed is read only when the private library has never contained a quote.

sections.title: 6. Quote display
sections.description: Control how approved quotes appear in the originating platform's chat.

sections.title: 7. Submission and moderation messages
sections.description: Short confirmations for successful library changes.

sections.title: 8. Help and error messages
sections.description: Clear responses for rejected or invalid requests.

sections.title: 9. Privacy and retention
sections.notice: Quote Vault stores only approved, pending, recently deleted quotes, their platform-scoped submitter identity, and a bounded moderation history. Ordinary chat is not retained. Deleted quotes remain recoverable only in a small rotating trash list. No data leaves the local computer except the quote responses deliberately sent to live chat.
